Gyro-Trac Marketing · June 2026 Plan

Our June Plan ·
The Chuck Leavell Launch
+ Cutter Head Push

A full-month plan covering Chuck's June 2 launch, the pivot to GT-25 cutter heads and refurbished mulchers, Dealer of the Month, Tim-led video, the trade show, and July 4th prep.

Launch · June 213-min Interview · 8 ShortsEmail · 10,000 ContactsSales Push · Cutter Heads + TeethRefurb GT-25 · Captive + Steel Track
1

Where We Stand

May 19 Sales · May 27 Marketing

Closed Out · May 19 Sales Meeting

Lead list — sentMarketing leads organized and handed to sales for follow-up.
Customer list — sentUpdated customer contacts delivered to the team for outreach.
Interview footage — chopped & editedAll assets in the Google Drive 2026 folder. 17 shorts cut. Final interview edit is 13 minutes.
Campaign data — reviewedPrior campaign: 140 opens / 81 clicks. Strongest engagement in SC + the Southeast.

Aligned · May 27 Marketing Meeting

June pivot · GT-25 over chipperRefurbished GT-25 captive + steel track mulchers and the cutter head lineup are the June sales engine. Chipper takes a back seat.
Blog is the traffic hubAll social and email funnels route to the blog landing page to capture leads — not directly to YouTube. YouTube hosts the video; the blog converts.
Launch date · June 2Pushed one day from June 1 to allow Chuck's team final approval. Blog + YouTube go live Tuesday morning.
Email · 10,000-contact sendFull list gets Email 1 (Legacy / Chuck), segmented re-send for Email 2 (cutter heads + parts).
Dealer of the Month series · adoptedRecurring content to strengthen partners + create FOMO for new dealer interest. Tim selects who's first.
Tim-led professional video · adoptedSales correlate with Tim on camera. Coordinate a real produced shoot, in addition to ongoing phone-recorded content.
Trade show prep · 100 flyersFull product line, no specific dealer info. Event name still needs confirming — notes said "rodeo, Columbia SC" but web searches don't surface a real match. Likely a transcription error.
July 4th campaign · parts-focused brainstormPlanning kicks off in W3 so creative is ready before the holiday.

Still in Motion · Awaiting

Chuck pre-approval — Daniel sending unlisted linkHard blocker for the June 2 launch. Needs confirmation before YouTube goes public.
Refurb serial numbers — OlivierNeeded to finalize the GT-25 mulcher listings on the website / app.
Ripping it Outdoors research — RyanInvestigating their CMI / Barco dealership partnerships and operating model. Report back at the meeting.
2

The Story We're Telling

Why this lands
“It's my
wife's fault.”

The hook isn't equipment specs — it's a Rolling Stones keyboardist who's also a National Outstanding Tree Farmer of the Year. A 53-year marriage, a family heritage of land stewardship, and the idea that "instruments come from wood, so I give back to the forest." That emotional spine carries the whole launch. The machine is the proof, not the pitch.

3

The Launch Rollout

3 Phases
June 2
AM

Phase 1 · Blog — The Traffic Hub

This is where every funnel points. Social + email all route here. Three-beat story (the unlikely origin, the stewardship philosophy, the GT-25 delivery), the YouTube video embedded inside, lead-capture form, and CTAs to the refurb GT-25 mulchers and cutter heads. Drafting next once approved.

June 2
AM

Phase 2 · YouTube — Where the Video Lives

The 13-minute interview hosted publicly so the blog can embed it. Title: "Why a Rolling Stones Legend Bought a Gyro-Trac." Thumbnail: Chuck + Daniel by the machine. Description points back to the blog — every comment pin links to the blog, not the YouTube channel.

June
3–10

Phase 3 · Social Shorts — The Drip

We don't dump all 17. 8 hand-picked clips drip out daily, strongest first. Every CTA links to the blog — that's the conversion point, not YouTube. See the lineup below.

4

The 8 Shorts We Run

From 17 → 8
DateClipWhy it makes the cut
June 3Chuck Leavell — "Music legend, forestry guru"The scroll-stopper. Identity reveal — strongest possible opener.
June 4From Music to Land StewardThe emotional "why." Highly shareable.
June 5Trees Grow From Air, Not SoilSurprising fact — drives comments and shares.
June 6Award-Winning Tree FarmerCredibility punch.
June 7Low-Impact Fire Clears BrushFirst real product-benefit clip.
June 8Mulching MachinesProduct education — natural bridge into the cutter head push.
June 9Wood: Heart of Your Favorite InstrumentsPoetic — ties the whole story together.
June 10Turn Waste Into WealthBusiness / ROI angle for buyers — sets up the sales push.
Hold as B-library:  Quail Hunting · Kubota Skid Steer · Meet Gabe · 42 Years Strong · Introducing My Wife · "WE" · America's Forests TV · From Musician to Land. Good clips — just niche or redundant with stronger picks. Save them for filler weeks.
5

How Each Platform Handles It

The Blog

The destination. Where every funnel terminates. Lead capture lives here. Embedded video, story copy, and CTAs to the GT-25 mulchers and cutter heads.

YouTube

Hosts the 13-minute interview, posted June 2 AM. Description + pinned comment both link back to the blog.

Facebook / Instagram

Reels, vertical, captions burned in for sound-off viewing. IG also gets 2–3 still pulls as feed posts. All CTAs link to the blog.

LinkedIn

Where this over-performs. B2B framing — "what a music legend taught us about land stewardship." 3 strongest clips spaced across 10 days. Our dealer + government buyers live here.

Before anything goes public: Chuck's team reviews the material first. Daniel is sending the unlisted YouTube link. Approval has to land before Tuesday morning or we push the launch. This is the one true blocker.

6

The June Calendar — Story + Sales

Two threads, one month

The team's new June priority is driving cutter head and parts sales. So Chuck's launch carries the awareness side, and cutter heads, replacement teeth, and the two refurbished GT-25 dedicated mulchers carry the sales side. The chipper stays in the rotation but isn't the headline.

W1Launch

StoryChuck launch owns the week · June 2–8

YouTube + blog drop June 2. Shorts drip Days 3–8. Sales content stays quiet — we don't split attention while the launch is finding its audience. Tim-video shoot scheduled this week if possible.

W2Bridge

StorySalesFinal Chuck shorts, cutter heads warm up · June 9–15

Last two Chuck shorts finish out (Mulching Machines, Wood/Instruments, Turn Waste). Sales side opens with the "parts where you need them" message — replacement cutter teeth available online OR at your dealer. Tease the two refurb GT-25 mulchers coming. First Dealer of the Month post lands this week.

W3Push

SalesCutter heads + refurb mulcher reveal · June 16–22

Cutter head attachments in the spotlight. Hero content on the lineup, demo clips, what each head is built for. Reveal the two refurb GT-25 dedicated mulchers (captive track + steel track) once they're live on the website's refurbished equipment page. Soft ADPRO social proof (confirm with Tim). July 4th creative brainstorm kicks off this week.

W4Convert

StorySalesTie story to sale, convert, set up July · June 23–30

Bring it together: the stewardship story Chuck told + the tools that do the work. CTA-heavy posts — quote a head, order teeth, book a mulcher demo. Targets the warm list built across the month. July 4th parts campaign creative finalized for early-July send. Trade show prep wraps (assuming late-June / early-July event).

7

On Deck — Two Refurbished GT-25 Dedicated Mulchers

Website work required

Two dedicated GT-25 mulcher units are coming to market through our refurbished equipment channel. Factory photography isn't ready yet, so we start promoting using existing refurb imagery and route all traffic to the refurbished equipment section. Once they're live, they anchor the W3 cutter head push.

GT-25 · Captive Track

Dedicated mulcher unit. Captive track configuration. Listing on the refurb page. Specs, photos (refurb imagery as placeholder), and CTA.

GT-25 · Steel Track

Dedicated mulcher unit. Steel track configuration. Listing on the refurb page. Specs, photos (refurb imagery as placeholder), and CTA.

Actions: 1) Olivier — share refurb serial numbers so we can finalize listings. 2) Web update on both units before June 16 — flag to Wendy / dev. 3) Once live, we hit them across W3 social and Email 2.

8

Other June Initiatives

From the May 27 marketing meeting

Dealer of the Month

Recurring content series across social + email. Strengthens current partners and creates FOMO that pulls new dealer interest. Tim picks who's first.

Tim-Led Pro Video

Sales correlate with Tim on camera. Coordinate a real produced session — better lighting, better setup, scripted talking points. Runs alongside the ongoing phone-recorded content. Ryan + Tim to schedule.

Trade Show · Columbia, SC

100 flyers, full product line, no specific dealer info. Confirm event name + date. Notes said "rodeo" — web search didn't find a match. Patricia / Tim to verify before we print.

July 4th · Parts-Focused

Brainstorm in W3, creative built in W4, send right before the holiday. Parts angle: replacement teeth + the "keep your iron running this summer" pitch. Ties cleanly into the cutter head push.

9

June Email — Two Sends to 10,000

Early June · ~June 4

Email 1 · The "Legacy" Send

"A Rolling Stones legend just bought a Gyro-Trac"
  • The Chuck interview — story-led, low pressure.
  • CTA links to the blog (which embeds the video).
  • Goes to the full 10,000-contact list — re-warms everyone.
  • Raises open rates so Email 2 lands stronger.
Mid–Late June · ~June 18–22

Email 2 · The Cutter Head & Parts Send

"Keep cutting. Parts where you need them."
  • Cutter head attachments — what each is built for.
  • Replacement teeth available online OR at your dealer.
  • Announce the two refurb GT-25 mulchers (if live).
  • Soft Dealer-of-the-Month slot baked in.
  • Hard CTA: quote a head, order teeth, book a demo.
  • Segmented: high-intent openers + SC / Southeast leads.

Why story first: the interview email warms the relationship; the cutter head email sells the iron. Story raises open rates, so the sales send reaches a hotter list.

What We Need From This Meeting

  1. Green light · June 2 launch — sign off on the blog-first rollout and the 8-short lineup so Ryan can finish the blog and queue the posts. Chuck's pre-approval still required (Daniel).
  2. Olivier — share the refurb serial numbers so we can finalize the GT-25 mulcher listings.
  3. Wendy / Dev — get the two refurb GT-25 mulchers (captive + steel track) live on the website by June 16 so they anchor the W3 push.
  4. Patricia / Tim — confirm the trade show. Notes said "rodeo, Columbia SC" — web search couldn't verify. Need real event name + date before we print 100 flyers.
  5. Tim — pick the first Dealer of the Month feature, and OK to name ADPRO as social proof in the W3 cutter head push?
  6. Ryan + Tim — lock a date for the Tim-led pro video shoot. Earlier in June = more sales lift across the month.
  7. Sales team — any specific cutter head models or teeth SKUs to feature first? We'll prioritize what's moving.
  8. Ryan — Ripping it Outdoors (CMI / Barco) research report-out at the meeting.
Gyro-Trac · Forestry & Land Management · June 2026 Marketing Plan
